title: viscum whitei subsp. whitei
intro: Viscum whitei subsp. whitei, also known as the New Zealand mistletoe, is a parasitic plant native to New Zealand. It has been found to possess medicinal properties, with studies indicating its potential in treating various health issues, including cancer, inflammation, and cardiovascular diseases. The plant's extracts have been shown to exhibit immunomodulatory, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant effects, providing potential health benefits such as improved immune function, reduced oxidative stress, and protection against cell damage. Viscum whitei subsp. whitei is commonly used in herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, and is also used in traditional medicine to support overall well-being.
